name: Semiconductors (SMH/SOX)
summary: Day 68 Wednesday. **SMH $522.69 (+3.1% 1D, +32.0% 1M, +30.1% 3M) — AI-CYCLE STRUCTURAL DECOUPLING FROM WEST ASIA RISK CONFIRMED.** Apple-Samsung-Intel chip-production exploratory talks (CNBC, Yahoo Finance, MarketScreener) — potential diversification beyond TSMC — drove KOSPI +6.7% to record 7,400.32 with Samsung crossing $1 TRILLION market cap (only second Korean company after SK Hynix to cross ₩1,000 trillion). SK Hynix +10%. Combined Samsung + SK Hynix concentration hit RECORD 42.2% of total KOSPI. Taiwan TAIEX 41,138.85 (+0.9% 1D, +26.3% 1M, +29.4% 3M) — TSMC+Samsung halo. The May 4-6 sequence — multi-axis Iran kinetic restart May 4 + Korean-flagged HMM Namu damaged in Hormuz + KOSPI +6.7% to record May 6 with Samsung $1T — is the cleanest STRUCTURAL VALIDATION of the AI-cycle decoupling thesis the May 3-5 briefs introduced. Apple flat $280 on May 4 multi-axis kinetic-restart day was the strongest single-day signal for AI-cycle resilience; KOSPI 7,000 + Samsung $1T = the structural confirmation on the Asian export side. Patel/SemiAnalysis bifurcation thesis sharpens: Apple/Microsoft cash strength vs Alphabet/Meta/Amazon at capex-OCF parity continues to outperform; relative US/US-tech allocation strengthens versus Indian-equity allocation specifically because Indian equities remain INR-FII-exposed while US/Korea/Taiwan tech is structurally decoupled from West Asia geopolitical risk. **For deployment thesis:** AI capex cycle is the dominant flow; crisis dynamics SECONDARY. Bifurcation thesis: long Korea/Taiwan/US tech AI vs short Indian IT services (which are NOT direct AI exposure, see indian-it node).
